siteDirectory.accessTabCrudNote: When a `canManage*` flag is true for a role, we document write/update/delete as allowed for that domain. `canView*` and audit access are read-first. For screen-level CRUD, use the full table at the top of the Structure tab.
siteDirectory.intro: Shows admin (`/admin`), customer (`/dashboard`, etc.), guide (`/guide`), and staff nav routes (schedule, reservations outside admin). Admin sidebar stays in sync with `admin-site-registry`.
siteDirectory.fullCrudTableIntro: Rows are grouped (e.g. header quick links vs sidebar). CRUD columns are Customer · Guide · OP · Office Manager · Super. Baselines come from code; values merge with Supabase `site_access_matrix_overrides`. Super and Office Manager can save in edit mode to drive admin sidebar and header quick-link visibility. Tabs and modals are indented by level.
siteDirectory.columnInMyMenuHint: Whether this item appears on the admin header/sidebar (customer/guide paths are not admin menu — shown as —)
siteDirectory.roleMatrixCaption: Flags by app role (`UserRole`). Database access is still enforced by Supabase RLS and some per-page checks.
siteDirectory.footnoteRls: Row-level security and `team.position` checks on individual pages can further restrict data. This matrix reflects app-level role flags used for navigation and features.
siteDirectory.ruleAlways: Any user in the admin layout
siteDirectory.matrixEditHint: Each cell uses Inherit / Yes / No. Page-level `hasPermission` and RLS are unchanged; denying read may hide links but not block direct URLs.
siteDirectory.structureFootnote: Rows with a light yellow background are hidden from your sidebar for the signed-in account. Dynamic routes (e.g. reservation detail `/admin/reservations/[id]`) are not listed here.
siteDirectory.treeIntro: Kinds: cluster (group) · page · in-page tab · modal · dynamic route. R/C/U/D = read · write(create) · update · delete (documentation level; RLS and per-page checks win).

balanceTable.formulaExplainerBody: Same DB fields as the reservation form “Pricing”. Formula total = effective product_price_total (incl. not-included where stored) − (coupon + add. discount) + (add. cost + tax + card fee + prepay cost + prepay tip + private add-on − refund) + (required options + optional options; live reservation_options sum overrides optional when present). choices_total and channel commission columns are not in this line. Compare reservation_pricing.total_price (DB) to the formula total. Channel block: settlement = channel payment − commission (same as Pricing); total revenue uses channel settlement base + not-included, OTA options, tax, etc. (computeCompanyTotalRevenueLikePricingSection); operating profit = total revenue − prepayment tip (same as section ④ in Pricing).

Total revenue and operating profit are not stored columns; after update they match the displayed formulas.

To use in Gmail:
1. Click "..." in the compose window
2. Select "Edit HTML"

If you see 550 / Message-ID: this is not caused by the HTML body. It happens when a mail client submits via Gmail SMTP without a valid RFC 5322 Message-ID header. form.cogsVsExpensesManualOpenTitle: When to use COGS vs operating expenses
form.cogsVsExpensesManualDialogTitle: Similar items: COGS vs operating expenses
form.cogsVsExpensesManualDialogDescription: Reference only when picking a standard line. Books and tax treatment follow your contracts, policy, and your CPA.

cogsVsExpensesManualContent.summaryText: COGS (CAT024 family) is for costs that attach directly to delivering a specific tour or a customer-facing promise. General expenses (transport, meals, office, etc.) are closer to running the business day-to-day.

examples.label: Fuel
examples.cogs: Gas for the vehicle used only to run that tour → e.g. “Fuel — tour execution vehicles.”
examples.opex: Office runs or general fleet → Transportation / vehicle operating lines.

examples.label: Meals
examples.cogs: Bentos or meals you give customers as part of the product → COGS (customer-provided meals).
examples.opex: Guides or staff eating on the job → Business meals / meals operating lines.

examples.label: Admissions / show tickets
examples.cogs: Pass-through or wholesale bundled into what you sell → COGS / admission-style lines.
examples.opex: Staff training or internal visits → Operating admission / travel lines.

examples.label: Guides / labor
examples.cogs: Day-rate or outsourced labor tied to one departure → COGS-style guide outsourcing.
examples.opex: Salaries or standing contractors → Payroll / operating.

examples.label: Vehicles
examples.cogs: Tour-only rental or costs used only to execute that tour → COGS.
examples.opex: Insurance, maintenance, depreciation for the whole business → Vehicle / maintenance operating lines.

cogsVsExpensesManualContent.noteText: For ticket “agency,” principal vs agent changes whether the ticket is COGS or only your fee is revenue—confirm with your advisor. If a label is ambiguous, use the rules above, then adjust mappings in the category manager if needed.

standardLeafDoubleCheck.bentoCogsBody:
This maps to IRS Schedule C Line 4 cost of goods sold. Use it only for the direct cost of meals or bentos provided to tour customers.

Easy to confuse with:
• Business meals (guide/staff) — when guides or employees eat as a business meal line.
• Tour customer meals — the standard “tour customer meals” line (often not COGS).

Confirm only if this matches your receipt.
standardLeafDoubleCheck.guideBusinessMealsBody:
This is closest to meals for guides or staff as a business meal expense.

Contrast with:
• Bento / meals (customer-provided) (COGS) — direct cost of food given to customers.
• Tour customer meals — when you intentionally book under the tour-customer meals line.

Double-check that the spend is really guide/staff meals, then continue.
standardLeafDoubleCheck.tourCustomerMealsBody:
This is for meals provided to customers during a tour under the meals / tour-customer path.

Contrast with:
• Bento / meals (customer-provided) (COGS) — same customer-facing meal, but classified as COGS / Line 4 depending on policy.
• Business meals — meals for guides or staff themselves.

Confirm whether this should be COGS or the tour-customer meals line, then continue.

adminReceiptOcrParseRules.receiptNoMediaHint: This row is «Receipt Pending» but media paths are empty. Check the tour expense record or upload/DB pipeline.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.receiptPreviewClickToExpand: Click to view larger · zoom in/out
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideStepBodyMatch: Body override: “If the full OCR text contains this phrase, force Paid-to / Paid-for” — e.g. horseshoe bend → Horseshoe Bend + Entrance Fee.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideStepCategory: Category: “If the receipt text contains these words, set Paid-for to …” — e.g. if you see costco, suggest Groceries.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideStepSkip: Merchant skip: “If a line contains this phrase, don’t treat that line as the store name” — e.g. thank you, www.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideStepAmount: Amount line: “Look for the dollar amount first on lines containing …” — e.g. Total, Grand total. If none match, the built-in logic still runs.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideFooter: Use “Body override” for venue-specific receipts. Other rules: add from templates, then Save. Plain phrases are enough; regex is optional under Advanced.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideWorkflowStep1: Pick a real receipt and run OCR, or paste OCR text manually.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideWorkflowStep2: Review the parse result, then use “Quick add from OCR” to create a body override rule.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideWorkflowStep3: Save and test again with the same receipt to confirm amount, vendor, and category.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.quickAddHint: Review the values below, then add a body override rule in one step. Click Save to apply.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.quickAddBodyMatchAdded: Rule added. Click Save to apply.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.quickAddPhraseRequired: Enter a phrase to match in the receipt text.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.quickAddTargetsRequired: Enter paid-to, paid-for, or enable the CC option.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.categoryPickHint: Pick from registered categories or type a custom name
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.matchingRulesSortedHint: Rules matching the preview OCR are shown first.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionBodyMatchHint: If the whole OCR text contains the phrase (case-insensitive, spaces normalized), the first matching rule applies. Leave Paid-to, Paid-for, or Payment method blank to keep the automatic guess / card matching.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionBodyMatchPlain: Put more specific phrases higher in the list. The Horseshoe Bend example is under Templates → Body override.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyContains: Phrase contained in full OCR text
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyPaidToPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep auto guess
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyPaidForPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep category keywords
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyPaymentMethodNone: Leave blank to keep card/text matching
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyPaymentCc: If last 4 card digits are found, set payment label to “CC”
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionCategoryPlain: Prefer several short keywords so shaky OCR still matches (mix English/Korean if needed).
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionPaidToSkipPlain: Each rule is tested against a whole line. Open Advanced to edit raw regex if needed.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionAmountPlain: If you add multiple hints, the parser tries those lines first for a dollar amount, then falls back to defaults.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.plainPhraseLabel: Phrase contained in the line (skip as merchant candidate)
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.plainPhraseLabelAmount: Phrase contained in the line (find amount here first)
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.plainPhraseHint: Type normal text only; matching is case-insensitive. It is stored as a safe pattern automatically.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.plainPhraseHintAmount: Short words that usually appear next to the total (Total, Amount due, etc.).
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.legacyPatternHint: This rule was saved as regex only. The plain box may be empty — check Advanced or re-type the phrase.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateDialogIntro: Pick a rule type, then tap a preset. “Body override” pins merchant + paid-for for specific venues.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateKindLabel: What do you want to add?
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateKindSkip: Line to exclude from merchant guess
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateBodyExplain: When the phrase appears anywhere in the OCR text, Paid-to and/or Paid-for are overwritten. Use this when a keyword like “Parking” would win over “Entrance Fee”.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateAddBodyMatch: Add body override with these values
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateCategoryExplain: If the OCR text contains these keywords, the Paid-for field is prefilled with the label.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateSkipExplain: Lines containing this phrase won’t be used as the store-name candidate.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateAmountExplain: The parser looks for a dollar amount on lines that contain this phrase first.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateAddLineRule: Add line rule with this phrase
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldKeywordsExplain: Separate with commas or newlines. Mix EN/KO so OCR typos still match when possible.

adminOperatorBManual.title: Operator B manual
adminOperatorBManual.description: Multi-tenant structure, completed work, next pilot tasks, and key admin screens in one place.
adminOperatorBManual.structureHint: Shared DB + operator_id. Kovegas is Tenant #1; Operator B is the second-supplier pilot.

tenancy.title: Tenancy
tenancy.body: operators / operator_members. Switch active operator in the header. Catalog, reservations, and customers are scoped by operator_id.

commerce.title: Commerce Core
commerce.body: Products, channels, dynamic pricing, bookings. OTA has outbox (push) plus inbound webhook inbox. v2 Offer/Inventory cut over via flags.

public.title: Customer site
public.body: Host → public operator (subdomain/custom domain). Catalog, Direct channel, and checkout stamp that tenant.

payments.title: Payments (Stripe Connect)
payments.body: After Express onboarding, enable destination charges with COMMERCE_STRIPE_CONNECT_CHECKOUT. Default remains platform PI.

ops.title: Operations Suite
ops.body: Fleet, attendance, expenses, cash, and statements appear only when modules.operations=true. When OFF, direct Ops URLs redirect here. Phase 6b stamps operator_id on fleet/finance tables; 6c.0 adds smoke checks.

adminOperatorBManual.todoHint: Re-evaluates DB/env for the tenant selected in the header. Green check = pass.
adminOperatorBManual.todoKovegasHint: You are on Kovegas. Switch to Operator B for meaningful Connect/catalog/Ops OFF scores.
adminOperatorBManual.activeOperatorSessionHint: Mirrors header tenant to cookie, middleware header, and API GUC. Staff SELECT lock-down is not applied yet.
adminOperatorBManual.activeOperatorSessionCheckBody: Pass after switching tenant once so the cookie syncs as a UUID. Outline badge if missing.
adminOperatorBManual.staffTenantLockCheckTitle: Staff tenant lock pilot (ops finance + journal)
adminOperatorBManual.staffTenantLockCheckBody: Only when SAAS_STAFF_TENANT_LOCK=1: stamp JWT claim and scope ops finance + journal staff SELECT. Default OFF.
adminOperatorBManual.opsBlockedBanner: Operations module is off, so Ops page URLs are blocked. You were redirected here.
adminOperatorBManual.regressionHint: Read-only checks that Tenant #1 baselines still hold. Booking/payment logic is not modified. All items should be green.
adminOperatorBManual.opsFinanceHint: Read-only: no NULL operator_id on fleet/expense/cash/statement/match/payment tables. Red if Phase 6b migrations are not applied.
adminOperatorBManual.commerceIsolationHint: Read-only stamp/partition checks for catalog, v2 offers, inventory, Direct resolve, and cross-tenant join samples. Does not call checkout or payment APIs.
adminOperatorBManual.hostBookHint: Read-only preflight before manual host book: subdomain, host resolve, catalog, Direct. Does not call checkout APIs.

subdomainConfigured.title: Subdomain configured
subdomainConfigured.body: Operator B requires operators.subdomain. Kovegas uses apex/localhost → Tenant #1.

routingEnvReady.title: Routing env
routingEnvReady.body: SAAS_PLATFORM_ROOT_DOMAIN / SUBDOMAIN_MAP / APEX. Local pilot may use sub.localhost.

hostResolveMatches.title: Host → operator resolve
hostResolveMatches.body: For B: sub.localhost must resolve to that operator_id.

debugEndpointAvailable.title: Debug endpoint note
debugEndpointAvailable.body: Non-prod: confirm middleware stamp via /api/debug/public-operator on B host.

stampRatePlans.title: rate_plans stamp
stampRatePlans.body: commerce v2 rate_plans operator_id NOT NULL.

stampOffers.title: offers stamp
stampOffers.body: commerce v2 offers operator_id NOT NULL (empty table still passes if no NULLs).

kovegasCatalogBaseline.title: Kovegas catalog baseline
kovegasCatalogBaseline.body: Tenant #1 products must not be empty.

tenantPartitionProducts.title: Tenant partition (products)
tenantPartitionProducts.body: Active tenant slice ≤ total. B UI must not list Kovegas products.

tenantPartitionChannels.title: Tenant partition (channels)
tenantPartitionChannels.body: B channels are B-scoped only. Kovegas Direct stays M00001.

connectCheckoutFlag.title: Connect checkout flag
connectCheckoutFlag.body: Kovegas stays on platform PI. For B, flag state is informational (OFF still passes isolation smoke).

stampInventoryCore.title: inventory core stamp
stampInventoryCore.body: inventory_resources / allotments / bindings operator_id NOT NULL (Phase 6e.1).

stampInventoryHoldsLedger.title: inventory holds/ledger stamp
stampInventoryHoldsLedger.body: inventory_holds and inventory_ledger operator_id NOT NULL (empty tables still pass if no NULLs).

channelProductSameTenant.title: channel_products ↔ product/channel same tenant
channelProductSameTenant.body: Sample-check channel_products.operator_id matches product and channel.

kovegasPaymentBaseline.title: Kovegas payment baseline
kovegasPaymentBaseline.body: Tenant #1 payment_records must not be empty.

tenantPartitionPayments.title: Tenant partition (payments)
tenantPartitionPayments.body: Active-tenant slice ≤ total. As B, deposit lists must not show Kovegas rows.

paymentRecordsSelectRls.title: payment_records SELECT RLS
paymentRecordsSelectRls.body: staff / operator_members / tour assignee / reservation customer SELECT (Phase 6c.1). Writes and payment logic unchanged.

opsFinanceSelectRls.title: Ops finance SELECT RLS
opsFinanceSelectRls.body: cash / accounts / statement / match SELECT include operator_members (Phase 6c.2). Writes and uploader privileges unchanged.

companyExpensesSelectRls.title: company_expenses SELECT RLS
companyExpensesSelectRls.body: staff session OR operator_members SELECT (Phase 6c.3). Write policies unchanged.

stampJournal.title: journal_* stamp
stampJournal.body: journal_entries and journal_lines operator_id NOT NULL (Phase 6c.4).

journalSelectRls.title: journal_* SELECT RLS
journalSelectRls.body: Journal header/line SELECT include operator_members (Phase 6c.4). Card-payment API stamp only.

fleetSelectRls.title: Fleet SELECT RLS
fleetSelectRls.body: vehicles / maintenance / schedules SELECT include operator_members (Phase 6c.5).

attendanceSelectRls.title: Attendance SELECT RLS
attendanceSelectRls.body: Own / platform admin / non-staff tenant owner·admin (Phase 6c.6). Uses is_operator_member_strict — Kovegas staff privacy kept.

tipMealSelectRls.title: Tip/Meal SELECT RLS
tipMealSelectRls.body: Meal / office tips / tip shares get non-staff tenant HR SELECT (Phase 6c.7). Existing staff/own policies kept.

tourResExpensesSelectRls.title: Tour/Reservation expenses SELECT RLS
tourResExpensesSelectRls.body: staff / operator_members / assignee SELECT (Phase 6c.8). Write policies unchanged.

staffTenantLockPilot.title: Staff tenant lock pilot
staffTenantLockPilot.body: Ops finance + journal SELECT use staff_can_select_operator_row (Phase 6d.3). Without JWT claim, staff stay all-tenant.

kovegasRow.title: Kovegas operator row
kovegasRow.body: The well-known operator_id row must exist.

kovegasOpsOn.title: Operations module ON
kovegasOpsOn.body: Kovegas continues to use fleet/expense Ops menus.

m00001Channel.title: M00001 Homepage channel
m00001Channel.body: Direct Web homepage channel row must exist.

directChannelM00001.title: Direct channel resolve = M00001
directChannelM00001.body: Public checkout for Kovegas must keep using M00001.

kovegasCatalog.title: Kovegas product catalog
kovegasCatalog.body: Existing products must remain under Kovegas operator_id.

platformCheckoutDefault.title: Checkout default = platform PI
platformCheckoutDefault.body: Connect flag must not force destination charges for Kovegas.

otaLiveDefaultOff.title: OTA LIVE default OFF
otaLiveDefaultOff.body: With COMMERCE_V2_OTA_LIVE off, behavior stays dry-run.

otaInboundDefaultOff.title: OTA INBOUND default OFF
otaInboundDefaultOff.body: Auto inquiry creation stays disabled by default.

commerceV2ReadNotGlobal.title: Commerce v2 read not global
commerceV2ReadNotGlobal.body: Unless COMMERCE_V2_READ_PRODUCTS=*, legacy RPC pricing remains default.

adminOperatorBManual.safetyHint: All SaaS features are opt-in. With flags unset, Kovegas operations match the previous system.

flagsOff.title: New flags default OFF
flagsOff.body: Connect checkout / OTA LIVE / OTA INBOUND / Commerce v2 read·inventory do nothing unless env is set.

kovegasUnchanged.title: Kovegas = Tenant #1
kovegasUnchanged.body: Existing products, reservations, channels, and Ops UI stay under Kovegas. No big-bang cutover.

m00001.title: Homepage = M00001
m00001.body: Customer Direct channel for Kovegas is always M00001. Only Operator B uses DW-slug style channels.

opsOptional.title: Operations is optional
opsOptional.body: Ops menus/URLs hide only for commerce-only B. Kovegas keeps operations=true.

pilotOptIn.title: Pilot is opt-in
pilotOptIn.body: Operator B create, Connect, subdomain, and checkout flags apply only after you enable them.

adminOperatorBManual.e2eTitle: Operator B E2E order (minimize legacy impact)
adminOperatorBManual.e2eHint: Run in this order, then switch back to Kovegas and confirm the regression badge stays green.

switchB.title: Switch header to Operator B
switchB.body: Ops menus disappear; product list is B-scoped.

catalogPrice.title: B products & dynamic pricing
catalogPrice.body: Add B-only catalog/Direct prices. Do not edit Kovegas rows.

commerceIsolationSmoke.title: Commerce isolation smoke all green
commerceIsolationSmoke.body: Confirm the Commerce isolation card on this page passes (stamp, partition, Direct).

hostBookReadiness.title: Host book readiness all green
hostBookReadiness.body: Confirm the Host book readiness card passes, then open the B host.

hostBook.title: Book on B host
hostBook.body: Confirm public operator is B via subdomain/map, then test booking or inquiry.

connectOptIn.title: Connect only when needed
connectOptIn.body: Put only B’s uuid in COMMERCE_STRIPE_CONNECT_CHECKOUT — avoid global *.

opsFinanceSmoke.title: Ops finance smoke all green
opsFinanceSmoke.body: Confirm the Ops finance card on this page passes (after Phase 6b migrations).

opsFinanceUi.title: B cash / statement / deposits UI
opsFinanceUi.body: As B (or Ops-ON tenant), Cash/Statement must not show Kovegas-only rows. If Ops OFF, only confirm menus are hidden.

switchBack.title: Switch back to Kovegas
switchBack.body: Ops and the legacy catalog must look unchanged.

regressionGreen.title: Confirm regression badge
regressionGreen.body: Refresh this page; «Kovegas regression» should be all pass.

createOperator.title: Create Operator B & invite members
createOperator.body: Create tenant → invite owner/admin → switch in the header.

connect.title: Finish Stripe Connect
connect.body: Start Connect → complete Dashboard onboarding → status=enabled.

subdomain.title: Subdomain / host mapping
subdomain.body: Set operators.subdomain plus SAAS_PLATFORM_ROOT_DOMAIN / map env. Verify via /api/debug/public-operator.

catalog.title: Products, channels, Direct channel
catalog.body: Create products as B. Missing Homepage/Website channel is auto-ensured as a DW-slug channel on checkout.

pricing.title: Enter dynamic pricing
pricing.body: Without dynamic_pricing on B’s Direct channel, the calendar may stay open and prices may use catalog_fallback.

checkoutFlag.title: Connect checkout flag
checkoutFlag.body: Set COMMERCE_STRIPE_CONNECT_CHECKOUT to Operator B’s UUID, book on B’s host, confirm connect_mode=destination.

isolation.title: Isolation checks
isolation.body: As B, Kovegas catalog/reservations must not appear; B host catalog must be B-only.

opsOff.title: Confirm Operations OFF
opsOff.body: With modules.operations=false, fleet/expense Ops menus must disappear from the sidebar.

opsRouteGuard.title: Block direct Ops URLs
opsRouteGuard.body: With Ops OFF, opening /admin/vehicles (etc.) must redirect to this manual.

adminOperatorBManual.pagesHint: Operator B group and core commerce screens. Click a card to open it.

adminOperatorBManual.pageCustomersBody: Per-tenant customers. Same email can exist under different operator_id.

adminOperatorBManual.doneHostBookReadiness: Host book readiness: subdomain/host resolve/Direct preflight (Phase 6e.2)
